description: American politician (1922-2015)
description_wiki: John Paul Hammerschmidt (May 4, 1922 – April 1, 2015) was an American politician from the U.S. state of Arkansas.

description: American politician (1905–1995)
description_wiki: James William Fulbright (April 9, 1905 – February 9, 1995) was an American politician, academic, and statesman who represented Arkansas in the United States Senate from 1945 until his resignation in 1974.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Arkansas ( , AR-kən-saw) is a landlocked state in the West South Central region of the Southern United States.

description: president of the United States from 1969 to 1974 (1913–1994)
description_wiki: Richard Milhous Nixon (January 9, 1913 – April 22, 1994) was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974.

description: former US attorney general, Watergate felon (1913-1988)
description_wiki: John Newton Mitchell (September 15, 1913 – November 9, 1988) was the 67th attorney general of the United States under President Richard Nixon.

description: private university in New Haven, Connecticut
description_wiki: Yale University is a private Ivy League research university in New Haven, Connecticut, United States.

description: President of the United States from 1993 to 2001 (born 1946)
description_wiki: William Jefferson  Clinton (né Blythe III; born August 19, 1946) is an American politician and lawyer  who served as the 42nd president of the United States from 1993 to 2001.

description: city in Crawford County, Arkansas, United States
description_wiki: Van Buren ( van BURE-ən) is the second-largest city in the Fort Smith, Arkansas–Oklahoma Metropolitan Statistical Area and the county seat of Crawford County, Arkansas, United States.

description: county in Arkansas, United States
description_wiki: Crawford County is a county located in the Ozarks region of the U.S. state of Arkansas.

description: county in Arkansas, United States
description_wiki: Clark County is a county located in the south-central part of the U.S. state of Arkansas.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

surface_forms: Texas coordinator of the McGovern campaign
description: American historian and politician (1922–2012)
description_wiki: George Stanley McGovern (July 19, 1922 – October 21, 2012) was an American politician, diplomat, and historian from South Dakota who served in both chambers of the United States Congress as a member of the United States House of Representatives for two terms representing South Dakota's 1st congressional district from 1957 to 1961, the director of Food for Peace in 1961 and 1962 under John F. Kennedy, and a member of the United States Senate for three terms from 1963 to 1981.

description: upper house of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Senate is a chamber of the bicameral United States Congress; it is the upper house, and the U.S. House of Representatives is the lower house.

description: United States association of physicians and medical students
description_wiki: The American Medical Association (AMA) is an American professional association and lobbying group of physicians and medical students.

description: male given name
county seat and city in Garland County, Arkansas, USA
description_wiki: Hot Springs is a resort city in the state of Arkansas, United States, and the county seat of Garland County.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: American politician
description_wiki: Leo Patrick McLaughlin (June 5, 1888 – May 5, 1958) was an American politician who served as the mayor of Hot Springs, Arkansas, from 1927 to 1947, and in the Arkansas House of Representatives in 1911.

description: 34th governor of Arkansas (1912–2003)
description_wiki: Sidney Sanders McMath (June 14, 1912 – October 4, 2003) was a U.S. marine, attorney and the 34th governor of Arkansas from 1949 to 1953.

description: 36th governor of Arkansas (1910-1994)
description_wiki: Orval Eugene Faubus ( FAW-bəs; January 7, 1910 – December 14, 1994) was an American politician who served as the 36th governor of Arkansas from 1955 to 1967, as a member of the Democratic Party.

description: American politician (1934–2024)
description_wiki: David Hampton Pryor (August 29, 1934 – April 20, 2024) was an American politician who served as a representative for Arkansas's 4th congressional district from 1966 until 1973 and as a senator from Arkansas from 1979 until 1997.
